finding: The sealed-runner-build blocker emits a requiredCommand that is not runnable as printed. It renders the framework temp task id with dots (ATM-FRAMEWORK-TEMP-codex-gpt-5.4-mini) while the id actually minted by framework-mode claim normalizes dots to dashes (ATM-FRAMEWORK-TEMP-codex-gpt-5-4-mini), so copy-pasting returns task-missing. The command also assumes an active framework claim exists but does not mention that framework-mode claim must run first, nor that it requires --files.
expectedBehavior: requiredCommand should be copy-paste runnable: emit the normalized task id, and either include the prerequisite framework-mode claim step or state it explicitly as a precondition.
evidenceOrRepro: Build at 43c6d07af emitted requiredCommand with --task "ATM-FRAMEWORK-TEMP-codex-gpt-5.4-mini"; running it verbatim returned "task ATM-FRAMEWORK-TEMP-codex-gpt-5.4-mini is task-missing; runner-sync steward enqueue requires an active task". framework-mode claim --actor codex-gpt-5.4-mini minted taskId ATM-FRAMEWORK-TEMP-codex-gpt-5-4-mini; enqueue succeeded only with the dashed id. framework-mode claim also failed first with ATM_CLI_USAGE requiring --files.
followUp: Consumer: ATM-GOV-0218 (runner-sync self-hosting closed loop) and TASK-ERR-0002 (error and recovery contract) will address the requiredCommand-must-be-runnable and prerequisite-declaration surface. Still supplies TASK-SKL-0014 with concrete dogfood evidence for the tool-first framework temp claim route. Do not open a standalone card.
